name: ms365-tenant-manager
description: Comprehensive Microsoft 365 tenant administration skill for setup, configuration, user management, security policies, and organizational structure optimization for Global Administrators
capabilities: Generate comprehensive tenant setup checklists with DNS records and security baselines
Create PowerShell scripts for bulk user creation, modification, and offboarding
Build Conditional Access policies and security audit reports
Recommend license assignments based on user roles and departments
Produce compliance documentation for GDPR, HIPAA, and security frameworks
author: alirezarezvani
securitySummary: This is a legitimate Microsoft 365 administration skill. Static findings are false positives: PowerShell generation is expected functionality for M365 management, documentation backticks are formatting not execution, URLs point to official Microsoft resources, and security references discuss enabling protections not implementing weaknesses.
valueStatement: Microsoft 365 tenant management requires careful configuration across multiple services. This skill provides automated guidance, security policies, and PowerShell scripts for Global Administrators managing users, licenses, security, and compliance.
limitations: Requires Global Administrator or appropriate role-based permissions to execute generated scripts
Cannot access tenant directly - generates scripts and guidance for manual implementation
PowerShell modules must be installed locally (Microsoft.Graph, ExchangeOnlineManagement, MicrosoftTeams)
Some compliance features require specific license tiers (E3, E5)

useCases.title: Automate tenant configuration
useCases.description: Generate complete setup guides and PowerShell scripts for new Microsoft 365 tenants with security best practices.

useCases.title: Implement access policies
useCases.description: Create Conditional Access policies requiring MFA and generate security audit scripts for compliance reporting.

useCases.title: Streamline user lifecycle
useCases.description: Produce bulk user provisioning and offboarding scripts with license management and mailbox handling.

promptTemplates.title: New tenant setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up a new Microsoft 365 environment
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete Microsoft 365 tenant setup guide for a 50-person company including DNS records, security baseline, and recommended license distribution.

promptTemplates.title: Bulk user creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a PowerShell script to create 25 new users from a CSV file and assign Microsoft 365 Business Standard licenses to each.

promptTemplates.title: Security hardening
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a security audit script that checks MFA status, admin role assignments, inactive users, and Conditional Access policies.

promptTemplates.title: License optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze license usage and recommend cost optimization for 100 users while maintaining necessary features for each department.

bestPractices: Always test generated scripts in a non-production environment before running them in production
Review and understand each script before execution - the skill generates scripts for admin review
Use Azure Key Vault or managed identities for authentication, never hardcode credentials in scripts
antiPatterns: Running generated scripts without reading and understanding what each command does
Executing scripts with Global Administrator when more limited permissions would suffice
Skipping the dry-run mode (-WhatIf) when available for destructive operations

faq.question: Does this skill execute PowerShell commands directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates PowerShell scripts and guidance as output. You review the scripts before running them yourself.

faq.question: What permissions do I need to run the generated scripts?
faq.answer: Permissions vary by task. User management needs User Administrator. Security policies need Security Administrator. Full tenant setup needs Global Administrator.

faq.question: Can this skill connect to my tenant?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates scripts and documentation. You run the scripts locally with your credentials after reviewing them.

faq.question: Which PowerShell modules are required?
faq.answer: The main modules are Microsoft.Graph for Graph API, ExchangeOnlineManagement for Exchange, and MicrosoftTeams for Teams administration.

faq.question: Is this skill compliant with GDPR and HIPAA?
faq.answer: The skill can generate compliance-focused configurations and reports. Actual compliance depends on your specific implementation and policies.

faq.question: Can I use this for hybrid AD environments?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on cloud-only scenarios. Hybrid environments require additional AD Connect configuration not covered by this skill.
